MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen E. Carlson of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000), appearing for moving parties: specially appearing Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. as alleged insurers of defendant Williams and Burrows Inc.

Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Robert Ross (274099), Michael Steinberger (274642), Willie Howard (275138), Monroe Amey (275535) and Joan Valladon as wrongful death heir and as successor-in-interest to George Valladon, deceased, et al. (421945).

RULING:
SPECIALLY APPEARING HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, NATIONWIDE INSURANCE CO.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST WILLIAMS AND BURROWS INC.

On the record the parties stipulated to argue all the motions in the following cases at the same hearing: Robert Ross (274099), Michael Steinberger (274642), Willie Howard (275138), Monroe Amey (275535) and George Valladon (421945).

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Specially appearing Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co.'s motion to set aside the default and default judgment entered against defendant Williams & Burrows, Inc. is granted in part and denied in part. Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. demonstrated that the Court should invoke its inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake because they demonstrated a meritorious defense, a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action, and diligence in seeking to set aside the default judgment once it was discovered. Therefore the default judgment entered against Williams & Burrows, Inc. on July 30, 2015 is set aside solely as related to Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. The motion to set aside the entry of default is denied. With the default judgment set aside, it is unnecessary to set aside the entry of default because an insured's default has no effect on the insurer(s). (Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.). Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Janice H. Dispo of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13299 (Tel: 510-451-1580). (503/CML)

Off Calendar Oct-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Monroe Amey

RULING:
INTERVENORS HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, WEST AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY, SAFECO INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, TRAVELERS INDEMNITY CO., ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE CO SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY, US BRANCH'S (ON BEHALF OF THEIR INSURED, DEFENDANT RICH-TEX, INC.) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenors' (on behalf of their insured, defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.) motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and punitive damages). Intervenors failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Intervenors did not satisfy their burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to Rich-Tex, Inc.'s special interrogatories and Rich-Tex, Inc.'s responses to standard interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Intervenors did not satisfy their burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claims against Rich-Tex. When read in conjunction with plaintiff's deposition testimony, Mr. Watkins' declaration merely creates a triable issue whether plaintiff worked with a product attributable to Rich-Tex. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Carol L. Schilp of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 9648 (503/TLJ)
